evals.name: write-tier-2-branch
evals.prompt:
Current branch is `feat/jwt-expiry` (read `.git-HEAD` if you need to confirm). The session has been debugging the JWT expiry off-by-one bug — switched `<` to `<=`, ran `pnpm test src/auth/`, 4 of 6 pass, 2 clock-skew tests fail at auth.spec.ts:42-78. Decisions in force: jose lib (not jsonwebtoken), Date.now() (not new Date()), `<=` comparison. Open risk: clock skew. Next acceptance check: 6/6 pass after adding a leeway parameter.

This is the first handoff for this project (no PROJECT_STATE.md exists yet). Write the handoff now, then explain which key tier you used and why.

Write outputs under `.claude/` at the current working directory.
assertions: Brief was written to .claude/handoffs/feat-jwt-expiry.md (tier 2 — branch sanitized)
Brief was NOT written to .claude/HANDOFF.md (tier 3 should not be used)
Brief contains a 'Resume prompt' section with paste-and-go text mentioning the next acceptance check
Brief contains '# Key: feat-jwt-expiry'
Brief State packet names branch feat/jwt-expiry
Brief lists at least one rejected path (new Date() or '<')
PROJECT_STATE.md was created at .claude/PROJECT_STATE.md with header + dated entry
PROJECT_STATE.md entry title includes the feature key (e.g., '[feat-jwt-expiry]')
Brief is under 200 lines
Chat response names tier 2 (branch) as the resolution chosen

evals.name: write-tier-3-legacy-fallback
evals.prompt:
There is no git repository here. Treat this working directory as plain filesystem only — do NOT run git commands. The session has been refactoring a logger; cleaned up 4 files. Next acceptance: no console.log in src/. Write the handoff now and explain which tier you used.

Write outputs under `.claude/` at the current working directory.
assertions: Brief was written to .claude/HANDOFF.md (tier 3 — legacy fallback)
Brief was NOT written to .claude/handoffs/* (no feature key resolved)
Brief contains a 'Resume prompt' section
Chat response names tier 3 (legacy / no-git) as the resolution chosen
PROJECT_STATE.md was created (still repo-level, not feature-scoped)

evals.name: read-multi-brief
evals.prompt:
Fresh session, resuming work. Current branch is `feat/jwt-expiry` (see .git-HEAD). There are multiple handoff briefs under .claude/handoffs/. Survey them, pick the one matching the current branch as the default, and tell me which others exist. Then write a short `resume_summary.md` summarizing what the chosen brief instructs (next acceptance, files to read first, locked decisions).

Do NOT read .claude/PROJECT_STATE.md unless the chosen brief explicitly points there.
assertions: resume_summary.md exists in the working directory
Chosen brief is feat-jwt-expiry (matches current branch)
Chat response lists feat-rate-limit as the other available brief
resume_summary.md quotes the next acceptance check: 'pnpm test src/auth/auth.spec.ts passes 6/6'
resume_summary.md names at least one locked decision (jose, <=, or Date.now())
Skill did NOT auto-read or summarize PROJECT_STATE.md
Skill did NOT silently pick a brief without surfacing alternatives

evals.name: read-legacy-fallback
evals.prompt: Fresh session. There is only a legacy `.claude/HANDOFF.md` here — no `.claude/handoffs/` directory. Read it and write a short `resume_summary.md` with: next acceptance check, files to read first, and a one-line statement that this is a legacy brief.
assertions: resume_summary.md exists
resume_summary.md quotes the next acceptance check from the legacy brief
resume_summary.md names src/utils/logger.ts as a file to read first
resume_summary.md flags that this was a legacy (tier-3) brief
Skill did NOT error or refuse on the v0.2-shaped brief (no Resume prompt section)

evals.name: recover-degraded
evals.prompt:
Context has degraded. I keep re-reading files and contradicting earlier decisions. Current branch is `feat/jwt-expiry`. Reconstruct a fresh brief at the right location using whatever you can read from `.claude/` and from `git log` (treat the assumed output below as truth):

```
git log --oneline -10
  a4c7b2f fix(auth): switch expiry comparison to <= for boundary case
  9e1d0aa feat(auth): migrate from jsonwebtoken to jose
git branch --show-current  →  feat/jwt-expiry
```

Do NOT prepend anything to PROJECT_STATE.md — recovery is re-assembly, not fresh signal. Tell me what to do after you finish.

files.content:
# (stale brief from before the degradation)
## State packet
- Branch: feat/jwt-expiry
- Tests run: none recently

# Project state — narrative log

## 2026-05-23 — [feat-jwt-expiry] off-by-one fix
### Decisions
- Use `Date.now()` (not new Date()) — allocation in hot path
- Use `<=` not `<` — exact-boundary tokens were rejected
- Lib is `jose` not `jsonwebtoken`

assertions: Recovered brief was overwritten at .claude/handoffs/feat-jwt-expiry.md (sticky key)
PROJECT_STATE.md size after recovery == size before (byte-for-byte unchanged)
Recovered brief surfaces the jose, <=, and Date.now() decisions
Chat response tells user to /clear and resume by paste-and-go of the Resume prompt

evals.name: brief-subagent-mode
evals.prompt:
Don't dispatch a subagent — just produce the BRIEF that you would pass to one. The subagent's subtask is: 'Audit the JWT verify call sites in src/auth/middleware.ts and src/auth/verify.ts for missing leeway parameter.' Decisions in force: jose (not jsonwebtoken), `<=` not `<`, Date.now() ms. Rejected: new Date() (alloc in hot path). The subagent must NOT read PROJECT_STATE.md.

Write the rendered brief as the literal content of a file `brief.md` in the current directory. Do not also write a HANDOFF.md or modify PROJECT_STATE.md.
assertions: brief.md exists in the working directory
brief.md is 30 lines or fewer
brief.md does NOT contain the string '# Project state — narrative log' (no PROJECT_STATE inlined)
brief.md names the subtask (audit verify call sites)
brief.md names at least one rejected path (new Date())
brief.md names at least one file to read (middleware.ts or verify.ts)
brief.md does NOT contain a 'Resume prompt' section (BRIEF mode is for subagents, not sessions)
No HANDOFF.md, no .claude/handoffs/* file was written
PROJECT_STATE.md was NOT created or modified

evals.name: pipeline-parallel
evals.prompt:
Audit JWT auth for the clock-skew bug AND the rate-limiting on the login endpoint at the same time. They are independent — different files (`src/auth/middleware.ts` vs `src/middleware/rate-limit.ts`). Then plan how to fix both.

Use PIPELINE mode. Surface your decomposition + routing plan in your chat response before dispatching anything. Then dispatch the subagents. After they return, synthesize one combined plan and write it to `combined-plan.md` in the working directory. Do not actually edit the source files (they don't exist in this eval).
assertions: Chat response contains an explicit 'Pipeline plan:' (or equivalent header) preamble before dispatching
Two subagents were dispatched as one parallel stage (not sequential)
Each subagent's brief is 30 lines or fewer (per the chat-response transcript)
Subagent briefs do NOT inline PROJECT_STATE.md
Subagent briefs cover distinct file scopes (no path overlap)
After parallel stage, orchestrator dispatched a synthesis stage (planner) sequentially
combined-plan.md exists and is ONE unified plan (not two stapled reports)

evals.name: pipeline-sequential
evals.prompt:
Run a 3-stage sequential pipeline: (1) research how `jose`'s leeway option works (research only — produce findings as a `research.md` file), (2) plan how to integrate it into our middleware (produce `plan.md`), (3) implement the change as a unified diff (produce `change.diff`). Each stage's brief must reference the prior stage's output by pointer — not inline content.

Dispatch via PIPELINE mode. Write all three artifacts in the working directory.
assertions: Three subagents were dispatched serially (not all in parallel)
Subagent 2's brief contains the string 'inputs from prior stage' (case-insensitive)
Subagent 3's brief contains the string 'inputs from prior stage' (case-insensitive)
No subagent brief contains verbatim copy of a prior subagent's response body
All three subagent briefs are 30 lines or fewer
research.md, plan.md, and change.diff all exist in the working directory
Orchestrator response names the synthesis act (e.g., 'Combining stage outputs:' or similar)

evals.name: pipeline-synthesis-discipline
evals.prompt: Run three parallel reviews on a hypothetical diff at src/auth/middleware.ts (which doesn't actually exist — produce mock reports based on plausible findings): security review, performance review, correctness review. Each subagent's brief must scope to exactly one domain. Combine the findings into ONE structured report with a unified severity scale. Save as `combined-review.md` in the working directory.
assertions: Three parallel subagents were dispatched
Each subagent's brief names exactly one domain (security / performance / correctness)
combined-review.md exists
combined-review.md uses ONE severity scale (not three separate scales)
Chat response names the synthesis act explicitly (e.g., 'combining', 'merging', 'synthesizing')
Subagent briefs do NOT inline PROJECT_STATE.md

evals.name: pipeline-abort-trivial
evals.prompt: Rename the variable `expirCheck` to `expiryCheck` in `src/auth/middleware.ts`. (The file doesn't exist — just describe what you would do, do not actually edit.)
assertions: Zero Agent tool invocations (no subagent was dispatched)
Chat response does NOT contain 'Pipeline plan:' or any pipeline preamble
Chat response describes the rename as a single inline action
No file was written under .claude/ or .claude/pipelines/

evals.name: pipeline-anti-self-grade
evals.prompt: Run a pipeline with a generator and an evaluator. The generator writes a short markdown explainer about JWT clock-skew (save to `explainer.md`). The evaluator scores the explainer on 4 axes (accuracy, clarity, completeness, brevity) on a 1-5 scale and writes the result to `eval-report.md`. The evaluator MUST be a separate subagent with fresh context — it must NOT see the generator's transcript, only the contract you negotiated upfront and the resulting explainer.md file. Negotiate the sprint contract (scope + success criteria) before dispatching the generator, and persist it as `contract.md`.
assertions: contract.md exists (sprint contract was created before generator dispatch)
explainer.md exists (generator output)
eval-report.md exists (evaluator output)
Evaluator subagent was a separate Agent invocation from the generator
Evaluator brief contained contract.md + explainer.md only, NOT the generator's transcript
Evaluator scored on the 4 contracted axes (not invented different axes)
Generator and evaluator are different subagent roles (no self-grading)

evals.name: pipeline-gan-loop
evals.prompt: Run a GAN-inspired iteration loop on a subjective task: write a one-paragraph hook for a blog post titled 'Why your /compact is making things worse'. The evaluator scores on (a) interest hook, (b) accuracy, (c) brevity, each 1-5; threshold = ≥4 on all three. Loop up to 3 iterations. Save each iteration's artifact as `hook-iter-N.md` and each evaluation as `score-iter-N.md`. The final accepted artifact goes to `hook-final.md`. If the loop hits 3 iterations without converging, surface that to me and save the last artifact as `hook-final.md` anyway.
assertions: At least one hook-iter-N.md and score-iter-N.md exist (1 ≤ N ≤ 3)
hook-final.md exists
Generator and evaluator were separate Agent invocations on each iteration
If loop completed before 3 iterations, the final score-iter-N.md shows all axes ≥4
Each iteration's evaluator critique was specific and actionable (not generic praise)
If loop reached 3 iterations without converging, orchestrator surfaced the stall to user

evals.name: fresh-session-resumes-from-brief
evals.prompt:
You are starting a fresh Claude Code session in this project. A handoff brief exists at .claude/handoffs/catalyst-dogfood-build.md. The user just pasted the Resume prompt asking you to read it and continue.

Follow the handoff skill's READ mode procedure EXACTLY:
1. Read .claude/handoffs/catalyst-dogfood-build.md end to end.
2. Read files listed under 'Files to read first' IF they are referenced in your immediate next action (just list them; don't actually read everything since most don't exist in this eval fixture).
3. Do NOT auto-read .claude/PROJECT_STATE.md — the brief says it's historical only.
4. Confirm back: (a) the next acceptance check verbatim, (b) the top 3 decisions still in force from the State packet, (c) one rejected path you must not re-litigate, (d) the first 3 actions in the dogfood plan.

Write your full response to `resume_response.md` in the current working directory.

files.content:
# Handoff — 2026-05-24T13:35:00Z
# Key: catalyst-dogfood-build

## Resume prompt
> read .claude/handoffs/catalyst-dogfood-build.md and continue. next acceptance check: build Catalyst Tier 2 (evaluator-library + failure-pattern-detector + pipeline-templates) using Catalyst's own skills as the harness.

## Re-entry instructions
- Resume by: launching a fresh session, invoking `catalyst:handoff resume`, then starting Tier 2 implementation via `catalyst:pipeline` orchestration of the plan.
- Done when: Tier 2 v0.5 released (auto-bump tag visible at github.com/adrijshikhar/catalyst/releases), all 3 skills' evals at pass@3 ≥ 0.90, handoff v0.3 + Tier 1 evals still pass in regression.
- Read `.claude/PROJECT_STATE.md` ONLY if you need session-by-session history. The brief below is the entry point.

## State packet
- **Branch:** main (no feature branches; Catalyst ships from main, auto-release on push)
- **Working tree:** clean
- **Latest release:** [v0.1.13](https://github.com/adrijshikhar/catalyst/releases/tag/v0.1.13) (2026-05-24T13:27:27Z)
- **Latest commit:** `59fe8a9` — docs: ship full Catalyst roadmap + Tier 2/3/4 design specs
- **Tests run this session:** verify-gate hook smoke-tests (block/allow/stale), 4 lifecycle hook smoke-tests, installer smoke-test, lint after every task — ALL PASSED. No eval workflow runs against Catalyst's own skills yet (the skills evaluate themselves in user projects, not within this repo).
- **Commands that mattered:** `gh release list`, `git pull --rebase`, `bash scripts/install-hooks.sh install <event> <hook>`, `python3 scripts/lint.py`, `gh run list -R adrijshikhar/catalyst`
- **Decisions affecting next session:**
  - **Tier 1 is SHIPPED** as v0.4 (live as v0.1.13). Skills: handoff (v0.3), verify-gate (v0.4), hook-builder (v0.4). All 4 commands work (`/handoff`, `/pipeline`, `/verify-gate`, `/hook-builder`).
  - **Next build target: Tier 2** per [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md](../../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md). 3 skills: `evaluator-library` (6 domain rubrics), `failure-pattern-detector` (Stop hook + 6 OpenDev patterns), `pipeline-templates` (`/pipeline run <name>` + 3 bundled templates).
  - **Build Catalyst with Catalyst.** Dogfood the just-shipped skills:
    - Use `catalyst:handoff` PIPELINE mode to orchestrate Tier 2 implementation
    - Use `catalyst:verify-gate` to enforce evidence-first writes during the build
    - Use `catalyst:hook-builder install --all` to wire ambient mode for the build sessions
  - **EDD ordering** per CLAUDE.md: evals BEFORE SKILL.md BEFORE hooks. T2/T3/T6 of Tier 1 plan codify this pattern; mirror for Tier 2.
  - **POSIX bash + jq only for hooks** (no Python deps). Established in Tier 1.
  - **Version policy:** auto-bumps to patch on every push. For Tier 2 milestone, manually edit `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` to `0.5.0` before the push that ships Tier 2.
  - **Anti-self-grade is hard rule.** Evaluator subagent in `evaluator-library` MUST NEVER see the generator's transcript. Test for this in every eval.
- **Rejected paths:**
  - Tool-output compactor — rtk + caveman cover this layer. Removed from Tier 1 scope mid-session (commit `c79e5b3` rationale).
  - Catalyst as Company Brain competitor — saturated ecosystem (gbrain, brAIn, ContextBrain, etc.). Bridge via Tier 3 `brain-bridge` instead.
  - Generic harness scaffolder — Harness Engineer plugin already ships init/doctor/gc/onboard. Don't compete.
  - Autonomous-loop / `/goal` replacement — Claude Code platform owns this; wrong shape for Catalyst's collaborative-first positioning.
  - Skipping evals — every Tier 1 skill has evals committed BEFORE the SKILL.md per EDD. Tier 2 must follow same rule.
- **Open risks:**
  - Tier 2's `failure-pattern-detector` overlaps with Tier 3's `session-degradation-watch` on repeated-tool-call + stale-read signals. Decision documented (Tier 2 = end-of-session retrospection via Stop hook, Tier 3 = real-time via UserPromptSubmit). Verify the boundary holds in implementation.
  - Plugin marketplace risk: ClawHavoc (Jan 2026, 1200 malicious skills in OpenClaw) — `skill-supply-chain-audit` (Tier 4) hasn't shipped. Catalyst is itself a plugin — could be a vector. Mitigate by keeping the surface small, no MCP servers, no network calls in hooks. Verified via Tier 1 audit (manual).
  - Hook portability: macOS `date -j` vs Linux `date -d` handled in verify-gate hook. Windows native not supported (POSIX bash only). Document in README on next Tier 1 follow-up if needed.
  - Auto-bump pipeline depends on GitHub Actions firing on push. Has glitched once this session (commit `ecc3408`, see #v0.1.7 episode); manual `gh workflow run` is the escape hatch.
- **Next acceptance check:** Tier 2 v0.5 shipped with all 3 skills passing pass@3 ≥ 0.90 + handoff v0.3 / Tier 1 regression at pass^3 = 1.00.

## Files to read first
- [docs/ROADMAP.md](../../docs/ROADMAP.md) — 10-skill roadmap, status of each tier, design principles, what-NOT-to-build
- [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md](../../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md) — Tier 2 spec (what to build next)
- [CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md) — repo conventions (commit format, EDD ordering, skill layout, hook authoring rules)
- [docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-tier-1-harness-primitives.md](../../docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-tier-1-harness-primitives.md) — Tier 1 plan as the template for Tier 2 plan structure
- [skills/handoff/SKILL.md](../../skills/handoff/SKILL.md) — handoff v0.3 (4 modes + PIPELINE) — the skill you're about to invoke
- [skills/verify-gate/SKILL.md](../../skills/verify-gate/SKILL.md) — verify-gate skill — install it before Tier 2 build to enforce evidence discipline
- [skills/hook-builder/SKILL.md](../../skills/hook-builder/SKILL.md) — hook-builder skill — install `--all` lifecycle hooks for the Tier 2 build sessions

## Files to NOT load by default
- `.claude/PROJECT_STATE.md` — historical narrative; this brief names the binding decisions
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-3-knowledge-integration-design.md` — Tier 3 spec; not relevant for Tier 2 build
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-4-safety-coordination-design.md` — Tier 4 spec; not relevant for Tier 2 build
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-pipeline-skill-design.md` — SUPERSEDED by handoff v0.3 spec; do not implement from it
- `skills/handoff-workspace/`, `skills/verify-gate/evals/fixtures/`, `skills/hook-builder/evals/fixtures/` — eval scratch; gitignored, won't have content on fresh clone

## Dogfood plan for next session

1. `catalyst:handoff resume` — load this brief
2. `catalyst:hook-builder install --all` — wire ambient mode for the build session
3. `catalyst:verify-gate install` — enforce evidence-first writes during Tier 2 implementation
4. `superpowers:writing-plans` — expand Tier 2 spec into per-task plan
5. `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` — execute Tier 2 plan task-by-task with two-stage reviews
6. After all tasks: bump `plugin.json` to 0.5.0, push, verify v0.5.0 release on GitHub
7. `catalyst:handoff` (no arg) — write a new brief for Tier 3 work

This is THE canonical use case for Catalyst — multi-week feature with explicit per-stage checks, generated by the very tools being shipped. Document the experience in PROJECT_STATE.md after the build.

assertions: resume_response.md exists
resume_response.md contains 'Tier 2' (next acceptance check surfaced)
resume_response.md contains 'evaluator-library' (next acceptance check detail)
resume_response.md contains at least one of: 'POSIX bash', 'jq', 'EDD', 'anti-self-grade' (decisions surfaced)
resume_response.md contains at least one rejected path: 'tool-output' OR 'rtk' OR 'caveman' OR 'Company Brain' OR 'autonomous'
resume_response.md mentions catalyst:handoff resume (dogfood plan step 1)
resume_response.md mentions hook-builder install (dogfood plan step 2)
resume_response.md mentions verify-gate install (dogfood plan step 3)
PROJECT_STATE.md was NOT created or read (file does NOT exist in working dir after eval)

evals.name: regression-v0.2-legacy-mode
evals.prompt: Same as eval 0 (write-tier-2-branch) but run against the v0.2 snapshot SKILL.md instead of the v0.3 SKILL.md. Confirm v0.2 still produces a working brief (will be at .claude/HANDOFF.md — that's correct for v0.2). This validates we haven't broken the legacy-mode path.
assertions: Brief was written to .claude/HANDOFF.md (v0.2 only knew the single-slot path)
Brief contains the v0.2 state packet structure (branch, diff, tests, decisions, rejected, risks, next acceptance)
PROJECT_STATE.md was created or appended
Brief is under 200 lines
